description_long: This is a heavy-duty pipe wrench with a robust, dark metallic head and a long, cylindrical wooden handle. The head features an adjustable jaw mechanism, with visible gear teeth and a knurled adjustment nut, designed for gripping and turning pipes. The handle is made of wood, showing signs of wear, discoloration, and some paint or residue, indicating extensive use. The metal head is dark, possibly steel or iron, with patches of rust and scratches, and the adjustment mechanism is clearly visible from the side views. The overall construction is solid, with the handle securely attached to the head, and the tool appears functional despite its worn appearance. This type of tool is commonly found in urban garages, workshops, or construction sites.
description: A worn pipe wrench with a dark metal adjustable head and a long, weathered wooden handle, showing signs of heavy use. The tool features a visible adjustment mechanism and rust patches.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular end of the handle and the broad, flat face of the wrench head with the adjustment nut visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the wooden handle and the detailed adjustable jaw mechanism of the wrench head.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the end of the handle and the back of the wrench head, with wear and discoloration on the handle clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the other profile of the wrench, with the adjustment nut and jaw teeth visible, and the handle's wood grain apparent.
attribute_handle_type: straight wooden handle, cylindrical, visibly worn with patches of discoloration and some paint residue, no ergonomic grip enhancements
attribute_head_type: adjustable pipe wrench head, made of dark steel, with a toothed jaw and knurled adjustment nut, no claw or striking face
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: significant wear on the handle, discoloration, paint residue, scratches, and rust patches on the metal head
